schema.base_allocation_in_yr: Annual per-acre allocation, inches. Null = trigger-based or none.
schema.allocation_cycle_in: Cycle total (inches) / years (e.g., 62.5/5 = 62.5 in over 5 yrs).
schema.notes: Special programs, carryforward caps, compact constraints.

districts.notes: Carryforward capped at 7.5" per period. Joint owner of NCORPE streamflow augmentation project. Allocation reduced from 13" in 2023.
Hard cap of 15" during NE/KS Compact Call years. Max 12" carryforward. Joint owner of NCORPE.
Hard cap of 13" during Compact Call years. Max 9" carryforward. Joint owner of NCORPE.
Spans three river basins; strict allocations applied only in the Republican River basin portion to comply with the tri-state compact.
Newest allocation in the state. Triggered automatically after two consecutive years of spring groundwater declines. Max 8" carryover.
Applies only to designated Quantity Management Subareas. Variable allocations by technology: Gravity 18", Subsurface Drip (SDI) 13", Center Pivot 14".
Operates a highly active, formalized water banking and trading program. Relies on groundwater decline triggers rather than district-wide hard allocations.
If water levels drop one foot below the 2016 baseline for two consecutive years, a strict 13" / 5-year allocation automatically triggers.
